name: cvss

metadata.name: cvss
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: CVSS2/3/4 library with interactive calculator for Python 2 and Python 3

documentation:
# CVSS

This Python package contains CVSS v2, v3 and v4 computation utilities and
interactive calculator (for v2 and v3 only) compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3.

The library is tested on all currently-supported Python versions available
via GitHub Actions (with the exception of Python 2.7, which is EOL but
still tested against), but it is simple enough to run on even older versions.

## Installation

```
# pip install cvss
```

## Usage

### Library

```python
from cvss import CVSS2, CVSS3, CVSS4


vector = 'AV:L/AC:L/Au:M/C:N/I:P/A:C/E:U/RL:W/RC:ND/CDP:L/TD:H/CR:ND/IR:ND/AR:M'
c = CVSS2(vector)
print(vector)
print(c.clean_vector())
print(c.scores())

print()

vector = 'CVSS:3.0/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N/AV:P/AC:H/PR:H/UI:R/E:H/RL:O/RC:R/CR:H/IR:X/AR:X/MAC:H/MPR:X/MUI:X/MC:L/MA:X'
c = CVSS3(vector)
print(vector)
print(c.clean_vector())
print(c.scores())
print(c.severities())

print()

vector = 'CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:H/SI:H/SA:N'
c = CVSS4(vector)
print(vector)
print(c.base_score)
print(c.severity)
```

Sample output:

```
AV:L/AC:L/Au:M/C:N/I:P/A:C/E:U/RL:W/RC:ND/CDP:L/TD:H/CR:ND/IR:ND/AR:M
AV:L/AC:L/Au:M/C:N/I:P/A:C/E:U/RL:W/CDP:L/TD:H/AR:M
(5.0, 4.0, 4.6)

CVSS:3.0/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N/AV:P/AC:H/PR:H/UI:R/E:H/RL:O/RC:R/CR:H/IR:X/AR:X/MAC:H/MPR:X/MUI:X/MC:L/MA:X
CVSS:3.0/AV:P/AC:H/PR:H/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N/E:H/RL:O/RC:R/CR:H/MAC:H/MC:L
(6.5, 6.0, 5.3)
('Medium', 'Medium', 'Medium')

CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:H/SI:H/SA:N
9.9
Critical
```

### Interactive calculator

For interactive calculator run the following:

```
$ cvss_calculator
```

For help on the calculator options run:

```
$ cvss_calculator --help
```

## Testing

For extensive testing, the test vectors were generated using official
JavaScript generators and [cvsslib](https://github.com/ctxis/cvsslib).

To run all tests using all supported versions of Python 2 and Python 3 installed:

```
$ tox
$ tox -e py311   # Run tests using a specific version of Python
```

modules.name: cvss.cvss_calculator

Computes CVSS2/3 score either supplied on command line or interactively. By default it only takes
mandatory metrics and computes CVSS3.1.
Copyright (c) 2016 Red Hat, Inc.
Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

documentation: string padding for score names

Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSSError
documentation: General CVSS exception.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS2Error
documentation: General CVSS2 exception.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS2MalformedError
documentation: Exception for malformed input CVSS2 vectors.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS2MandatoryError
documentation: Exception for missing mandatory fields.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS2RHScoreDoesNotMatch
documentation:
Exception when parsing CVSS2 vectors in Red Hat notation, which have score not matching the
computed score.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS2RHMalformedError
documentation: Exception for malformed input CVSS2 vectors in Red Hat notation.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS3Error
documentation: General CVSS3 exception.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS3MalformedError
documentation: Exception for malformed input CVSS3 vectors.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS3MandatoryError
documentation: Exception for missing mandatory fields.

documentation:
Exception when parsing CVSS3 vectors in Red Hat notation, which have score not matching the
computed score.

documentation: Exception for malformed input CVSS3 vectors in Red Hat notation.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS4Error
documentation: General CVSS4 exception.

classes.name: cvss.exceptions.CVSS4MalformedError
documentation: Exception for malformed input CVSS4 vectors.

modules.name: cvss
documentation: Copyright (c) 2016 Red Hat, Inc.
Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

modules.name: cvss.interactive
documentation:
Functions for interactively building CVSS vector string.

Compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3.
Copyright (c) 2016 Red Hat, Inc.
Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

functions.name: cvss.interactive.color
documentation:
Replaces text in brackets with yellow bold variant for terminal. Also replaces | symbol with
blue one. Should improve readability. Uses ANSI Styling codes.

functions.name: cvss.interactive.ask_interactively
documentation:
Asks user to build CVSS vector string interactively.

Args:
    version (float): 2 or 3.0/3.1 or 4 for CVSS2 or CVSS3 or CVSS4 respectively
    all_metrics (bool): If true, temporal and environmental metrics are asked, else only base
                        metrics are asked for
    no_colors (bool): If true, terminal coloring is not used in interactive mode

Returns:
    (str): CVSS vector

modules.name: cvss.constants2
documentation: Constants for CVSS2 computations and checks. Generated using util/generate_constants.py.
Copyright (c) 2016 Red Hat, Inc.
Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

modules.name: cvss.cvss3
documentation:
Implements class for CVSS3 specification as defined at
https://www.first.org/cvss/specification-document .

The library is compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3.
Copyright (c) 2016 Red Hat, Inc.
Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

functions.name: cvss.cvss3.round_up
documentation:
Round up is defined as the smallest number, specified to one decimal place, that is equal to
or higher than its input. For example, Round up (4.02) is 4.1; and Round up (4.00) is 4.0.

classes.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.from_rh_vector
documentation:
Creates a CVSS3 object from CVSS vector in Red Hat notation, e.g. containing base score.
Also checks if the score matches the vector.

Args:
    vector (str): string specifying CVSS3 vector in Red Hat notation, fields may be out of
                  order, fields which are not mandatory may be missing

Returns:
    CVSS3: the generated CVSS3 object created from the vector string

Raises:
    CVSS3RHMalformedError: if vector is not in expected format for Red Hat notation
    CVSS3RHScoreDoesNotMatch: if vector and score do not match

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    vector (str): string specifying CVSS3 vector, fields may be out of order, fields which
                  are not mandatory may be missing

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.parse_vector
documentation:
Parses metrics from the CVSS3 vector.

Raises:
    CVSS3MalformedError: if vector is not in expected format

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.handle_scope
documentation: Sets scope and modified scope variables based on S and MS metrics

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.check_mandatory
documentation:
Checks if mandatory fields are in CVSS3 vector.

Raises:
    CVSS3MandatoryError: if mandatory metric is missing in the vector

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.add_missing_optional
documentation:
Adds missing optional parameters, so they match the mandatory ones. Original metrics are
also stored, as they may be used for printing back the minimal vector.

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.get_value
documentation:
Gets value of specific metric specified by its abbreviation. Handles exception for
Privileges Required based on Scope or Modified scope.

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.get_value_description
documentation: Gets textual description of specific metric specified by its abbreviation.

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_isc_base
documentation: ISCBase = 1 - [(1-ImpactConf) x (1-ImpactInteg) x (1-ImpactAvail)]

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_isc
documentation:
Scope Unchanged 6.42 x ISCBase
Scope Changed 7.52 x [ISCBase-0.029] - 3.25 x [ISCBase-0.02]^15

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_esc
documentation: 8.22 x AttackVector x AttackComplexity x PrivilegeRequired x UserInteraction

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_base_score
documentation:
If (Impact sub score =< 0) 0 else,
Scope Unchanged Round up (Minimum [(Impact + Exploitability),10])
Scope Changed Round up (Minimum [1.08 x (Impact + Exploitability),10])

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_temporal_score
documentation: Round up(BaseScore x ExploitCodeMaturity x RemediationLevel x ReportConfidence)

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_modified_isc_base
documentation: ISCModified = Minimum[[1-(1-M.IConf x CR)x(1-M.IInteg x IR)x(1-M.IAvail x AR)],0.915]

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_modified_isc_30
documentation:
This is CVSS:3.0 version

If Modified Scope Unchanged    6.42 x [ISCModified]
If Modified Scope Changed      7.52 x [ISCModified-0.029] - 3.25 x [ISCModified-0.02]^15

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_modified_isc
documentation:
This is CVSS:3.1 version

If Modified Scope Unchanged    6.42 x [ISCModified]
If Modified Scope Changed      7.52 x (ISCModified - 0.029) - 3.25 x
                               (ISCModified x 0.9731 - 0.02)^13

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_modified_esc
documentation: 8.22 x M.AttackVector x M.AttackComplexity x M.PrivilegeRequired x M.UserInteraction

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.compute_environmental_score
documentation:
If (Modified Impact Sub score =< 0)    0 else,
If Modified Scope Unchanged    Round up(Round up (Minimum [
                  (M.Impact + M.Exploitability),10])
                  x Exploit Code Maturity
                  x Remediation Level
                  x Report Confidence)

If Modified Scope Changed    Round up(Round up (Minimum [1.08
                  x (M.Impact + M.Exploitability),10])
                  x Exploit Code Maturity
                  x Remediation Level
                  x Report Confidence))

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.scores
documentation:
Returns all computed scores.

Returns:
    (tuple of floats): Base Score, Temporal Score, Environmental Score

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.clean_vector
documentation:
Returns vector without optional metrics marked as X and in preferred order.

Args:
    output_prefix (bool): defines if CVSS vector should be printed with prefix

Returns:
    (str): cleaned CVSS3 with metrics in correct order

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.severities
documentation:
Returns severities based on scores.

Returns:
    (tuple): Base Severity, Temporal Severity, Environmental Severity as strings

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.rh_vector
documentation:
Returns cleaned vector with score in Red Hat notation, e.g. score/vector.

Example: 6.5/CVSS:3.0/AV:P/AC:H/PR:H/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N/E:H/RL:O/RC:R/CR:H/MAC:H/MC:L

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.temporal_vector
documentation:
Returns the temporal vector from the full CVSS vector.

Returns:
    (str): Temporal CVSS vector.

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.environmental_vector
documentation:
Returns the environmental vector from the full CVSS vector.

Returns:
    (str): Environmental CVSS vector.

methods.name: cvss.cvss3.CVSS3.as_json
documentation:
Returns a dictionary formatted with attribute names and values defined by the official
CVSS JSON schema:

CVSS v3.0: https://www.first.org/cvss/cvss-v3.0.json?20170531
CVSS v3.1: https://www.first.org/cvss/cvss-v3.1.json?20190610

Serialize a `cvss` instance to JSON with:

json.dumps(cvss.as_json())

Or get sorted JSON in an OrderedDict with:

json.dumps(cvss.as_json(sort=True))

Returns:
    (dict): JSON schema-compatible CVSS representation
Class to hold CVSS3 vector, parsed values, and all scores.

functions.name: cvss.parser.parse_cvss_from_text
documentation:
Parses CVSS2 and CVSS3 vectors from arbitrary text and returns a list of CVSS objects.

Parses text for substrings that look similar to CVSS vector
and feeds these matches to CVSS constructor.

Args:
    text (str): arbitrary text
Returns:
    A list of CVSS objects.

modules.name: cvss.cvss2
documentation:
Implements class for CVSS2 specification as defined at
https://www.first.org/cvss/v2/guide .

The library is compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3.
Copyright (c) 2016 Red Hat, Inc.
Author: Stanislav Kontar, Red Hat Product Security
License: LGPLv3+

functions.name: cvss.cvss2.round_to_1_decimal
documentation: Round to one decimal.

classes.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.from_rh_vector
documentation:
Creates a CVSS2 object from CVSS vector in Red Hat notation, e.g. containing base score.
Also checks if the score matches the vector.

Args:
    vector (str): string specifying CVSS3 vector in Red Hat notation, fields may be out of
                  order, fields which are not mandatory may be missing

Returns:
    CVSS2: the generated CVSS2 object created from the vector string

Raises:
    CVSS2RHMalformedError: if vector is not in expected format for Red Hat notation
    CVSS2RHScoreDoesNotMatch: if vector and score do not match

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    vector (str): string specifying CVSS2 vector, fields may be out of order, fields which
                  are not mandatory may be missing

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.parse_vector
documentation:
Parses metrics from the CVSS2 vector.

Raises:
    CVSS2MalformedError: if vector is not in expected format

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.check_mandatory
documentation:
Checks if mandatory fields are in CVSS2 vector.

Raises:
    CVSS2MandatoryError: if mandatory metric is missing in the vector

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.get_value
documentation: Gets value of specific metric specified by its abbreviation.

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.get_value_description
documentation: Gets textual description of specific metric specified by its abbreviation.

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.impact_equation
documentation: Impact = 10.41*(1-(1-ConfImpact)*(1-IntegImpact)*(1-AvailImpact))

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.adjusted_impact_equation
documentation:
AdjustedImpact = min(10,10.41*(1-(1-ConfImpact*ConfReq)*(1-IntegImpact*IntegReq)
                             *(1-AvailImpact*AvailReq)))

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.base_score_equation
documentation:
BaseScore = round_to_1_decimal(((0.6*Impact)+(0.4*Exploitability)-1.5)*f(Impact))
Impact = see impact_equation or modified_impact_equation
Exploitability = 20*AccessVector*AccessComplexity*Authentication
f(impact)= 0 if Impact=0, 1.176 otherwise

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.compute_base_score
documentation: Compute base score using normal Impact equation. Do not allow negative result.

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.temporal_score_equation
documentation:
TemporalScore = round_to_1_decimal(BaseScore*Exploitability
                *RemediationLevel*ReportConfidence)

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.compute_temporal_score
documentation: Compute temporal score using normal Impact equation.

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.compute_environmental_score
documentation:
EnvironmentalScore = round_to_1_decimal((AdjustedTemporal+
(10-AdjustedTemporal)*CollateralDamagePotential)*TargetDistribution)

AdjustedTemporal = TemporalScore recomputed with the BaseScores Impact sub-equation
                   replaced with the AdjustedImpact equation

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.scores
documentation:
Returns all computed scores.

Returns:
    (tuple): Base Score, Temporal Score, Environmental Score, either float or None if not
             defined

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.clean_vector
documentation:
Returns vector without optional metrics marked as ND and in preferred order.

Returns:
    (str): cleaned CVSS2 with metrics in correct order

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.severities
documentation:
Returns severities based on scores. https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln-metrics/cvss

Returns:
    (tuple): Base Severity, Temporal Severity, Environmental Severity as strings

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.rh_vector
documentation:
Returns cleaned vector with score in Red Hat notation, e.g. score/vector.

Example: 5.0/AV:L/AC:L/Au:M/C:N/I:P/A:C/E:U/RL:W/CDP:L/TD:H/AR:M

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.temporal_vector
documentation:
Returns the temporal vector from the full CVSS vector.

Returns:
    (str): Temporal CVSS vector.

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.environmental_vector
documentation:
Returns the environmental vector from the full CVSS vector.

Returns:
    (str): Environmental CVSS vector.

methods.name: cvss.cvss2.CVSS2.as_json
documentation:
Returns a dictionary formatted with attribute names and values defined by the official
CVSS JSON schema:

https://www.first.org/cvss/cvss-v2.0.json?20170531

Serialize a CVSS2 instance to JSON with:

json.dumps(cvss2.as_json())

Or get sorted JSON in an OrderedDict with:

json.dumps(cvss2.as_json(sort=True))

Returns:
    (dict): JSON schema-compatible CVSS representation
Class to hold CVSS2 vector, parsed values, and all scores.

modules.name: cvss.cvss4
documentation:
This class is a rewrite based on the JS implementation found here:
https://github.com/RedHatProductSecurity/cvss-v4-calculator

Implements class for CVSS4 specification as defined at
https://www.first.org/cvss/specification-document .

The library is compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3.

functions.name: cvss.cvss4.final_rounding
documentation:
Round to one decimal place. Use Decimal because Python float rounding defaults to
"round half to even". We actually want "round half away from zero" aka "round half up" for
positive numbers.

Add a small value to make sure that values like the following are correctly rounded despite
floating point inaccuracies:

8.6 - 7.15 = 1.4499999999999993 (float) => 1.5

Example: 7.3/CVSS:4.0/AV:P/AC:H/AT:N/PR:H/UI:P/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:H/SI:H/SA:H/E:A

modules.name: cvss.constants4
documentation:
Constants for CVSS4 computations and checks.
Translated from the RedHatProductSecurity cvss-v4-calculator repository:
https://github.com/RedHatProductSecurity/cvss-v4-calculator
